title: Remarks on generic stability and random types
abstract: We introduce the notions of $rgs$ and $irgs$ as properties of a Keisler measure $μ$, and prove that they are respectively equivalent to the existence of a generically stable random type that extends $μ$ and to the fact that its canonical extension, namely the random type $r_μ$, is generically stable. We compare these notions with the known concepts of $fim$, $fam$, and self-averaging, and in particular we show that every $irgs$ measure is dependent in the sense of [10].
comment: This is an initial version. Comments are welcome. k.khanaki @ gmail.com
